title: 10 best herbal capsules for sore eyes
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for sore eyes are a natural and effective solution to alleviate discomfort and irritation caused by dry, itchy, or tired eyes. These capsules contain a blend of herbal extracts that soothe and calm the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy vision. Examples include capsules containing ingredients like bilberry extract, eyebright, and chamomile, which have been shown to improve symptoms of conjunctivitis and dry eye syndrome. By using these herbal capsules, individuals can enjoy improved visual clarity, reduced redness and swelling, and enhanced overall eye health, allowing them to go about their daily lives with greater comfort and confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain the herb Euphrasia officinalis, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. They also help to loosen and flush out impurities that can cause discomfort. Additionally, eyebright's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright capsules contain flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from redness, itching, and burning.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in eyebright capsules help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome and other eye problems.

Strengthening and soothing mucous membrane properties: Eyebright contains compounds that help strengthen and soothe the mucous membranes in the eyes, reducing irritation and promoting healthy tear production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Eyebright roots are used because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.

Buds: Eyebright buds are employed due to their rich content of sesquiterpenes, which possess natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making them effective in treating conjunctivitis and other eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 250mg of euphrasia officinalis powder with 500mg of potato starch and fill 100 empty gelatin capsules for a single batch.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or until opened.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, glycyrrhizin is absorbed into the bloodstream and can help to reduce inflammation in the body, including in the eyes. This can provide relief from symptoms of dry eye syndrome, such as redness, itchiness, and burning sensations. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to soothe irritated tissues, making it a natural remedy for sore and tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort in the eyes caused by sore eyes or conjunctivitis.
Flavonoids: Licorice flavonoids have been shown to possess antiallergic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe itchy, irritated eyes and reduce inflammation.
Amino acids (glycine): Glycine has been found to have a protective effect on the cornea and conjunctiva, helping to repair damaged tissues and reduce eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to make medicinal capsules for sore eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce eye inflammation and relieve dryness.
remedy_recipe: Dry the dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 100 grams of the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered root with 500 milliliters of distilled water to create an herbal infusion and steep it for 30 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to collect the liquid extract and discard the solids.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the liquid extract and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with sore eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce redness and swelling. The herb's flavonoids and carotenoids also possess antioxidant properties that help combat oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals in the eyes. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial activity helps to eliminate bacterial infections that can cause eye irritation, promoting a healthy and soothing environment for the eyes. As a result, herbal pot marigold capsules provide natural relief from sore and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from redness and discomfort.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in pot marigold have been shown to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic eye conditions like age-related macular degeneration and cataracts.
Soften and lubricate mucus membranes: Pot marigold's mucilages can help soften and lubricate the mucus membranes in the eyes, providing moisture and relief to dry, itchy, or irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with sore eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the pot marigold plant contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help soothe and protect the delicate tissues of the eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom when petals are fully open and dry them immediately to preserve flavor.
Clean and sort dried flowers removing any debris or stems to ensure optimal potency.
Grind 20-30 grams of dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5-7 minutes.
Mix the ground calendula officinalis with 50-100 grams of vegetable capsules filling them 2/3 full with the herbal blend.
Store filled capsules in a cool dry place protected from light for up to 6 months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with sore eyes because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated ocular tissues. The herbal extract contains a unique combination of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that work together to reduce redness and swelling. Additionally, aloe's natural antioxidant activity helps protect the delicate eye tissues from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy vision and reducing discomfort associated with sore eyes. This natural remedy provides effective relief for individuals suffering from dry, itchy, or irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe capsules contain compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from redness, itchiness, and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal aloe capsules help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall eye health.
Soften and moisturizing properties: Herbal aloe capsules contain mucilages that help soften and soothe dry, irritated eyes, providing natural lubrication and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the eyes.
Buds: Contain high levels of glycoproteins that help to soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from a healthy plant free of pesticides and other contaminants weighing approximately 100 grams.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris taking about 10 minutes.
Dry the washed leaves in a low-temperature oven at 60 degrees celsius for two hours to remove excess moisture.
Crush the dried aloe vera leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle mixture consisting of one part aloe vera to three parts tapioca starch taking approximately 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared aloe vera powder in doses of 500 milligrams per capsule taking about 10 minutes for each batch of 100 capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which effectively soothe irritated eye tissues. The capsules also possess antimicrobial properties that help combat infections and reduce redness, making them an excellent natural remedy for relieving dry, itchy, or burning eyes. Additionally, the herb's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from further damage, promoting healthy vision and overall eye wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like esculetin and scutellarein, which help reduce swelling and pain in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of sore eyes.
Antioxidant properties: The herb's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health.
Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas coneflower has been shown to modulate the immune system, which can help regulate inflammation and reduce symptoms of sore eyes, such as redness and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots have been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and sterols, seeds help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the eye tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots and dry them properly for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 500mg of powdered echinacea angustifolia root with 100mg of silica gel as filler in a clean glass bowl.
Fill 500 empty capsules with the prepared herbal mixture and seal tightly for storage.
Label the finished capsules with expiration date, dosage instructions, and ingredient list and store in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with sore eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The capsule's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, work together to reduce swelling and inflammation, providing relief from dryness, itchiness, and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's natural antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from further damage, promoting overall ocular health and reducing the risk of chronic eye conditions. As a result, herbal dandelion capsules offer a safe and natural solution for alleviating sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate symptoms of soreness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in dandelion capsules have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce swelling and redness associated with sore eyes.
Diuretic properties: Dandelion's diuretic properties can help relieve water retention and reduce puffiness around the eyes, which can contribute to a sense of comfort and relief from soreness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Fruits: Contain powerful flavonoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in ocular tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers on a sunny day for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dry place for at least 24 hours or using a food dehydrator at 40°c.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, ensuring a uniform consistency.
Mix 500mg of the taraxacum officinale powder with 250ml of distilled water to create a concentrated herbal extract, stirring well for 15 minutes.
Fill #00 gelatin capsules with 200mg of the herbal extract mixture, sealing each capsule immediately after filling and storing them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with sore eyes because it contains hyperforin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound works to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from irritation and discomfort. Additionally, St John's Wort has antioxidant properties that help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By taking St John's Wort capsules regularly, individuals can enjoy natural and effective relief from sore and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sore eyes.
Hypotensive compounds: St. John's Wort contains various hypotensive agents that can help lower blood pressure in the eyes, reducing eye strain and discomfort.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by exposure to environmental toxins or other irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye irritation.
Barks: Rich in tannins and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that aid in soothing sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers at peak potency during summer months.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator.
Combine dried flowers with silica gel powder in a ratio of 4:1 by weight.
Fill empty capsules with the herb-silica mixture and seal immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain a rich source of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These natural ingredients help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, soothing irritated tissues and relieving discomfort. Additionally, the capsules' ability to strengthen blood vessels and promote healthy circulation also helps to reduce inflammation and prevent further irritation, making them a natural and effective solution for soothing sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness, irritation, and inflammation in the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in Maidenhair Tree capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sore eyes.

Vasoactive compounds: Ginkgo biloba's vasoactive compounds, such as ginkgolides and bilobalide, improve blood flow to the eyes, which can help to alleviate dryness, itchiness, and blurry vision caused by reduced blood circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in sore eyes.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, promoting healing and recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source.
Grind the ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 3 minutes.
Mix the ginkgo biloba powder with 200 grams of tapioca starch and 100 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill #00 empty capsules with the mixture, using approximately 250 milligrams per capsule, and seal them immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to eye irritation and dryness. By taking herbal ginger capsules, individuals can help soothe their eyes and reduce redness and itchiness, promoting healthy vision and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from soreness and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: The active compounds in ginger capsules, such as gingerols and paradol, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relieve irritation in the eyes.
Buds: Ginger buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure 500 milligrams of dried ginger root powder accurately.
Grind an additional 250 milliliters of fresh ginger rhizome into fine paste.
Mix the ground ginger paste with 200 milliliters of distilled water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to extract juice and discard solids.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the resulting ginger juice and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that reduces redness and swelling. The capsules also include antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated eyes, alleviating discomfort and fatigue caused by dryness, exposure to screens, or environmental irritants. Additionally, chamomile's natural sedative properties promote relaxation, calming the eyelids and surrounding skin, providing welcome relief for tired, sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sore eyes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in chamomile capsules protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye irritation.
Anxiolytic and sedative: Chamomile's calming effects help relax the muscles around the eyes, reduce tension, and promote a sense of calm, which can alleviate eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, an antioxidant with potent anti-inflammatory properties, flowers reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried chamomile flowers containing 80-100mg of essential oils and crush them into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 250mg of crushed chamomile powder with 125mg of pure vegetable filler in a bowl and mix well for 5 minutes.
Use gelatin capsules measuring 0.9mm in diameter and fill each one with approximately 375mg of the herbal blend for 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.
Take 2-3 capsules orally twice daily as needed for sore eyes, swallowing them whole with water and allowing 30-60 minutes for effects to take hold.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain a powerful antioxidant called saponin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The capsules also help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles around the eyes, providing relief from dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations. Additionally, fennel's natural antibacterial and antiviral properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to sore eyes. Overall, herbal fennel capsules offer a gentle and effective way to alleviate eye discomfort and promote healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel capsules contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from sore and irritated eyes.
Antioxidant: Fennel's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness, itchiness, and irritation, thereby soothing sore eyes.
Antispasmodic: Fennel's antispasmodic properties can help relax muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and discomfort that may be contributing to sore or strained eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making them effective in soothing sore eyes.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain essential oils that have antibacterial properties, which can help combat eye infections and promote healing of sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure 25 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems to prepare herbal formula.
Mix measured foeniculum vulgare with 50 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes to create decoction.
Strain decoction through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids after 5 minutes preparation time.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of dried foeniculum vulgare powder and seal tightly after 2 hours drying time.
Store herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year or until expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with sore eyes because it contains a unique phytoestrogen called agnuside, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling. The herb also has antispasmodic effects, helping to relax the muscles around the eye and alleviate dryness and itchiness associated with sore eyes. Additionally, chaste tree capsules can help regulate hormonal imbalances in the body, which may contribute to eye issues such as blurred vision and sensitivity to light.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Chasteberry (Vitex agnus-castus) has been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help increase blood flow and oxygenation to the eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with sore eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant activities, which can help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the eye tissues, reducing damage caused by UV radiation and other environmental factors.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Chasteberry has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with sore eyes, such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chasteberry roots are rich in flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Seeds: The seeds of the chaste tree contain essential fatty acids that nourish and moisturize dry, itchy eyes, promoting healthy vision.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried vitex agnus castus fruit from a trusted supplier or local market.
Grind the dried fruit into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 500mg of gelatin and 100mg of glycerin in a bowl to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 250mg of the herb mixture and seal tightly within 30 seconds.
Label and store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with sore eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. The curcumin present in turmeric has natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that help to soothe and calm irritated eyes. Additionally, it also exhibits antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infections that may be causing the soreness. By taking turmeric capsules regularly, you can experience relief from sore eyes and improve overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric capsules contain curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from sore eyes.
Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant compounds can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue, dryness, and irritation.

Immune-system support: Turmeric has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, meaning it can help regulate and modulate the immune response in the body, potentially helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, leading to improved overall eye health and reduced soreness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and reduce eye inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the turmeric plant is the main source of its active compounds, including curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried turmeric root (curcuma longa) into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix the ground turmeric powder with 250 grams of gelatin in a bowl until well combined and smooth.
Add 100 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin is fully dissolved and the mixture forms a thick paste.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with one teaspoon of the turmeric-gelatin mixture per capsule, using a mortar and pestle or spoon to fill each capsule tightly but not overfilling.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze them for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain allicin, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, garlic capsules can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from soreness and discomfort. Additionally, the antibacterial and antiviral properties of garlic can also help to combat eye infections that may be contributing to eye irritation. As a result, taking herbal garlic capsules may provide effective natural relief for sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Allicin: Allicin, a sulfur compound found in garlic, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce eye inflammation and alleviate symptoms of sore eyes such as redness and itchiness.

Vitamins and minerals: Herbal garlic capsules often contain vitamins C and E, as well as minerals like copper and zinc, which are essential for maintaining healthy vision and may help to reduce the risk of age-related macular degeneration, a common cause of sore eyes in older adults.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Garlic's natural antibacterial and antifungal properties can help combat infections that may be contributing to sore eyes, such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of garlic contain sulfur compounds and other bioactive molecules that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in sore eyes.
Buds: Garlic buds are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried allium sativum powder and vegetable capsules for filling.
Weigh out one tablespoon of dried allium sativum powder using a digital scale.
Fill each vegetarian capsule with the measured amount of powdered allium sativum and seal securely.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.
Take one to two capsules orally three times daily as needed or directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Monk's pepper capsules helps with sore eyes because of their unique combination of bioactive compounds, particularly the alkaloids which exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sore eyes, while also soothing and calming the eye surface. Additionally, Monk's pepper has been traditionally used to treat a range of ocular issues, including conjunctivitis and dry eye syndrome, making it a natural and effective remedy for promoting healthy, comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Monk's Pepper Capsules contain antioxidants that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to sore eyes.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes, making them effective for soothing sore or irritated eyes.

Antibacterial and Antiviral Properties: Monk's Pepper Capsules contain compounds that exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help combat eye infections and irritations caused by bacteria or viruses, promoting healing and reducing eye pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, Monk's Pepper roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in sore eyes.
Buds: The tender buds of the Monk's Pepper plant contain flavonoids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in soothing irritated eye tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried cnidium monnieri roots and stems.
Grind the harvested material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 milliliters of distilled water with 10 grams of the ground powder in a glass jar and let it steep at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids after 15 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the filtered liquid extract and store them in an airtight container to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Porter's lovage capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain a unique blend of natural herbal ingredients, including lovage root, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The capsules also include soothing herbs like chamomile and calendula, which reduce redness and swelling while promoting healthy tear production. This combination effectively relieves dry, itchy, and tired eyes, making it an excellent natural remedy for those struggling with sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lovage capsules contain compounds like isoimperatorin and kumatakenin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the eye, helping to alleviate redness and swelling associated with sore eyes.

Antioxidant properties: Lovage contains antioxidants like polyphenols and flavonoids, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic eye conditions like cataracts and macular degeneration.

Antimicrobial properties: The active compounds in lovage capsules have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria and viruses that can cause conjunctivitis and other eye infections, helping to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Lovage contain compounds like coumarins, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing eye inflammation.
Buds: The buds of Lovage contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce redness and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry ligusticum porteri leaves and stems for medicinal use.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the herb powder with filler and lubricant for easy capsule filling.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine or spoon.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container to preserve potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen capsules helps with sore eyes because of their unique composition. The gel-like extract from the leaves of the wintergreen plant, Gaultheria procumbens, contains methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever that closely resembles aspirin. When taken as a supplement, this compound reaches the blood and is then carried to the eyes, providing quick relief from soreness, inflammation, and itchiness caused by dryness or irritation. Its anti-inflammatory properties soothe tired eyes, making it an effective natural remedy for addressing common eye discomforts.
remedy_properties: Methyl Salicylate: Helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with sore eyes, as it has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties similar to aspirin.
Gingerols: Has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and discomfort.
Tannins: Acts as an astringent, helping to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the eyes by constricting blood vessels and reducing excess mucus production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in methyl salicylate, a key ingredient in relieving pain and inflammation associated with sore eyes.
Rhyzomes: Contain high concentrations of salicin, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems for optimal potency.
Cleanse the harvested material by gently removing dirt debris and contaminants.
Air-dry the cleansed material in a warm dry place for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the air-dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the ground powder for optimal dosing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's natural compounds work to reduce redness and swelling, while also promoting healthy blood flow to the eyes. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, leading to a reduction in discomfort and fatigue associated with sore eyes. As a result, rosemary capsules can provide relief from dry, itchy, or tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate symptoms of sore eyes such as redness and irritation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, including rosmarinic acid, may help reduce swelling and pain associated with eye infections or allergies that cause sore eyes.
Stimulant properties: Rosemary's stimulant effects can increase blood flow to the eyes, which can help to reduce congestion, dryness, and itchiness associated with sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to reduce redness and swelling in sore eyes.
Buds: Containing high levels of essential oils, which can help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves measuring exactly one ounce or twenty-eight grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for eight hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for five minutes.
Mix the ground herb with vegetable cellulose and water to create a gelatinous capsule filling using a ratio of one part herb to two parts filler.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with sore eyes because they contain flavonoids, a type of antioxidant that reduces inflammation and alleviates discomfort. The capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the affected area, providing quick relief from redness and itchiness. Additionally, maypop's natural ingredients help to calm the nerves, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can contribute to sore eyes in the first place. remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Maypop capsules contain flavonoids, which are antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye irritation by neutralizing free radicals.
Cytokinins: Cytokinins in maypop capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
Steroidal saponins: Steroidal saponins in maypop capsules have anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergic conjunctivitis and other eye irritations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, maypop roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Fruits: Maypop fruits contain flavonoids and other phytochemicals that have been shown to reduce eye inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves according to standard botanical guidelines.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm air circulation oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Crush dried passiflora incarnata plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part powdered passiflora incarnata with 5 parts vegetable filler such as silica or calcium carbonate and fill empty gelatin capsules.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sore eyes with herbal capsules is to first consult with an eye care professional to determine the underlying cause of the discomfort. Next, take the recommended dosage of a high-quality herbal supplement that contains soothing herbs such as eyebright, chamomile, or licorice root extract. These natural ingredients help reduce inflammation and alleviate dryness, promoting healthy vision and relieving sore eyes.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sore eyes that are treated with herbal capsules are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and dry eye syndrome. These conditions often exhibit symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and discharge, which can be alleviated by natural remedies like bilberry extract, eyebright, and chamomile. Herbal capsules containing these ingredients may help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy vision.
